mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: High unemployment rates indicate weak labor market conditions that suppress consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite high unemployment signals.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates robust corporate activity and demand for goods, boosting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth exceeds 1.5% but 20-day SPX returns remain negative in > 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation (CPIAUCSL) combined with declining GDP (GDPC1) signals stagflation, reducing corporate margins and investor confidence, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL > 2.5% and GDPC1 < 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) during late-cycle expansion signal continued demand for construction and consumer goods, driving corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Housing starts exceed 800k but 20-day SPX returns remain negative in > 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) combined with low unemployment (UNRATE) indicates a tight labor market that drives wage inflation and corporate profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 50% of instances where PAYEMS > 100k and UNRATE < 5%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS) combined with high inflation (CPIAUCSL) signals aggressive monetary tightening, increasing borrowing costs and reducing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where FEDFUNDS > 2% and CPIAUCSL > 3%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) and housing starts (HOUST) indicate strong economic activity, boosting corporate earnings and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 50% of instances where INDPRO and HOUST both rise by > 1%.
